question.content: In PI-based flow cytometry, fluorescence intensity is proportional to DNA content. The plots compare wild-type cells and gene-A-deficient mutant cells under no irradiation and gamma-irradiation conditions. Determine which cell-cycle phase contains most mutant cells after 12 hours of gamma irradiation. Write only one phase. Answer format: one final result only. If the result naturally simplifies to a single value, give that value; otherwise give a single algebraic expression using the symbols defined in the problem. Use ASCII characters only. Do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign. Answer format: a single cell-cycle phase name; write only the name itself, with no explanatory text, units, or equals sign.

step_1.content: Map PI fluorescence peaks to DNA-content states before comparison.
step_1.key_point: Left major peak corresponds to 2n and the right region corresponds to 4n.

step_3.content: Inspect the mutant profile after gamma irradiation and compare peak dominance.
step_3.key_point: Most cells shift from the 2n-dominant region to the 4n-dominant region.

step_5.content: A dominant 4n DNA-content peak indicates post-replication arrest.
step_5.key_point: PI DNA histograms identify this accumulation as G2 arrest.

step_7.content: Return only one compact phase expression (single phase only).
